text:
It was in 1972 that the aromatic π-sextet rule was introduced by Erich Clar. Forty years on this fairly qualitative model is still a major source of inspiration for the understanding of the aromaticity of benzenoid species. The validity of this model has been demonstrated by a large amount of experimental evidence: X ray analyses, NMR, STM and AFM microscopies, as well as by theoretical investigations, shown in this volume.

This perspective aims at explaining the longevity of Clar’s rule and shows its applicability in a variety of chemical situations. Its application to predict the local aromaticity and reactivity in polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) and also to understand two of the most recent developments in PAH chemistry, namely, resonance-assisted hydrogen bonding and the emerging biradical character in higher acenes are illustrated. Even with its known and sometimes unknown limitations, Clar’s rule remains a fundamental and useful tool for dealing with the sp2 hybridized carbon materials.
